prompt: Two-commit bug-fix branch fix/842-token-refresh-race against main. Repo history uses plain imperative subjects. Draft a PR title + body.

assertions.text: Title uses plain imperative style (NOT Conventional Commits) — repo history is non-conventional
Title mentions the bug being fixed (e.g., 'race', 'token refresh', 'concurrent')
Body contains a Summary describing the bug + fix at a system level (not file-by-file)
Body references issue #842 as Fixes #842 or Closes #842 (GitHub-style, picked up from branch name '842')
Body explains the root cause (duplicate refreshes / unsynchronized check-then-set)
Body has a Review notes section pointing to the regression test as a recommended starting point
Body does NOT enumerate the two commits (no 'Commits:' section, no per-commit headers)
Body does NOT contain tooling references ('Claude', 'AI-generated', 'verified by', test-output dumps)
Body is short — <= 25 lines for this bug-fix size
